next_commands: topogram query slice ./topo --mode implementation --projection proj_operations_ui_contract --detail compact --json
next_queries: topogram query slice ./topo --mode implementation --projection proj_operations_ui_contract --detail compact --json
agent_guidance.write_scope_summary: Edit the canonical Topogram source and project-owned files only; generated-owned outputs should be regenerated.

terms.description: A widget-first mapping from semantic widgets to platform component refs and behavior support for a design contract.
terms.name: Design Realization Set

terms.description: A reusable semantic template that composes regions for screens without prescribing DOM, SwiftUI, Android, or CSS layout code.
terms.name: Layout Contract

terms.description: A coordination app for monitoring intake, triage, assignment, active field work, incident review, and resolution.
terms.name: Operations Control Room

terms.description: A reusable semantic work area such as command bar, filter rail, results, detail summary, timeline, inspector, or footer actions.
terms.name: Region Contract

terms.description: A reusable semantic UI contract that can be realized by different platform components while keeping one product meaning.
terms.name: Widget

terms.description: A graph that tells humans and agents where product work belongs without pretending to be a framework render tree.
terms.name: Work Map

slice_manifest.note: Read must_read sections first; use reference sections only as needed; run proof commands before completing work.

sections.reason: The selected graph surface this packet is scoped to.
sections.title: Focus

sections.reason: The shortest human-readable description of the selected work or surface.
sections.title: Summary

sections.reason: Mode, read order, follow-up queries, and required proof commands.
sections.title: Agent Guidance

sections.reason: Canonical vocabulary needed to interpret this slice.
sections.title: Glossary Terms

sections.reason: Automation and human-review expectations for this scope.
sections.title: Review Boundary

sections.reason: Generated, maintained, and human-owned boundaries that constrain edits.
sections.title: Ownership Boundary

sections.reason: Files and surfaces that are safe, risky, or out of bounds to edit.
sections.title: Write Scope

sections.reason: Screen, layout, region, widget, accessibility, i18n, design-token, and UI proof context for UI slices.
sections.title: UI Agent Packet

sections.reason: IDs that define the semantic closure without embedding every full record.
sections.title: Depends On

sections.reason: Summaries for directly related graph records.
sections.title: Related Records

sections.reason: Compact counts and IDs for related records omitted from compact detail.
sections.title: Related Summary

sections.reason: Smallest verification set recommended for this change.
sections.title: Verification Targets

sections.reason: Verification records directly linked to this slice.
sections.title: Verification Records

summary.description: Shared UI contract for the operations-dashboard proof with reusable layouts, screens, navigation, and semantic widget bindings.
summary.name: Operations UI Contract

obligations: Prefer semantic role, accessible-name source, keyboard model, focus behavior, live-region need, and proof command over raw aria-* snapshots.
Treat raw aria-* attributes as generated or maintained implementation evidence, not the contract source.
accessibility.source: ui_contract accessibility block, design-token evidence, terms, decisions, and requirements

diagnostics: No design_contract mapping matches this focused UI scope; unsupported or custom component realization is developer/agent review work.
designContracts.source: design_contract records own design-system scope and token mappings; design_realization_set records map semantic widgets to platform component refs and behavior support.

designReview.source: ui-design-coverage design_matrix rows from design_contract and design_realization_set records

messages.defaultText: Incoming work that needs triage appears here.
Create or activate teams before assigning work.
Incoming work that needs triage appears here.
Create or activate teams before assigning work.

obligations: Use glossary terms for canonical meaning and message keys for translated wording.
Keep concrete locale catalogs in generated or maintained app code until a message contract DSL exists.
internationalization.source: ui_contract messages block, glossary terms, decisions, and requirements

requiredGates.reason: Validate shared UI ownership, taxonomy, references, and topology.
Validate widget placement, props, events, regions, and patterns.
Inspect behavior realizations and partial bindings before code changes.
Confirm screens, regions, widgets, display fields, behavior, and design tokens are realizable by the web generator.

diagnostics.message: Widget usage display fields could not be derived from a shape or capability output.
diagnostics.suggested_fix: Bind the widget data prop to a capability with an output shape or add item/view/input shape metadata on the screen.

verification.description: Runs proof path hygiene, proof audit, Topogram check, strict SDLC check, agent brief, commit prep, and clean-worktree verification for the first proof checkpoint.
verification.name: Step 01 Domain And App Shell Verification

verification.description: Runs proof path hygiene, proof audit, Topogram check, strict SDLC check, domain slice generation, region catalog review, and clean-worktree verification for the semantic region checkpoint.
verification.name: Step 02 Semantic Regions Verification

verification.description: Runs proof path hygiene, proof audit, Topogram check, strict SDLC check, domain slice generation, layout catalog review, and clean-worktree verification for the reusable layout checkpoint.
verification.name: Step 03 Reusable Layouts Verification

verification.description: Runs proof path hygiene, proof audit, Topogram check, strict SDLC check, screen slice generation, screen catalog review, and clean-worktree verification for the screen/navigation checkpoint.
verification.name: Step 04 Screens And Navigation Verification

verification.description: Runs proof path hygiene, proof audit, Topogram check, strict SDLC check, screen and widget slice generation, widget catalog review, and clean-worktree verification for the semantic widget binding checkpoint.
verification.name: Step 05 Widgets And Bindings Verification

verification.description: Runs proof path hygiene, proof audit, Topogram check, strict SDLC check, UI design coverage, UI realization report, focused widget slice review, and clean-worktree verification for the design realization checkpoint.
verification.name: Step 06 Design Contract Realizations Verification

verification.description: Runs proof path hygiene, proof audit, Topogram check, strict SDLC check, UI design coverage, UI realization report, focused screen/widget slices, and clean-worktree verification for i18n and accessibility obligations.
verification.name: Step 07 i18n ARIA And States Verification

verification.description: Runs proof path hygiene, proof audit, Topogram check, strict SDLC check, focused screen/widget/projection slice generation, slice usefulness review, and clean-worktree verification for the final agent slice checkpoint.
verification.name: Step 08 Agent Slice Review Verification

verification_targets.rationale: Projection slices affect generated contract and runtime surfaces, so verification should follow the projection closure.
